actions: Compare production tree to source commit excluding .audit/** and docs/audit/**; if different create SOURCE_DRIFT.md and rerun affected checks.
oracle: No unexplained production drift; conclusions explicitly revalidated.

actions: Run PowerShell parser/PSScriptAnalyzer on every tracked .ps1/.cmd wrapper from D:\jarvis-gpt without executing service actions.
oracle: All scripts parse; any analyzer suppression is reviewed.

actions: In a disposable launcher state and fake dispatcher identity, test absent, truncated and malformed launcher-state.json then invoke stop.
oracle: Unknown ownership fails closed and fake foreign dispatcher remains running.

actions: Start a harmless process whose command line mentions D:\jarvis-gpt but is not launcher-owned; invoke stop and inspect exact PID/birth identity.
oracle: Foreign process survives; only recorded descendants stop.

actions: Copy only launcher harness to temp paths containing spaces, Cyrillic and apostrophe; invoke status/doctor from another cwd.
oracle: Path resolution/quoting works and no unrelated path is touched.

actions: Render isolated Compose project twice: empty JARVIS_API_TOKEN, then a synthetic token; do not use user data or production ports.
oracle: Empty token gives actionable preflight failure; synthetic token yields authenticated proxy/backend path.

actions: Run docker compose config and an isolated startup using cached images on alternate project name/ports.
oracle: Backend has a valid health contract and frontend reaches ready deterministically.

actions: After inventorying cached assets, disconnect network safely and start/restart gemma4-turbo without --build; capture Docker events and DNS/registry attempts.
oracle: Cached turbo start does not pull or resolve registries; explicit network tools degrade independently.

actions: Repeat cached offline start for gemma4-mono-perf and capture resolved model/image/flags.
oracle: 31B perf profile uses intended model/4k flags and no downloads.

actions: Repeat cached offline start for gemma4-mono with watchdog and cleanup.
oracle: 31B mono profile uses intended model/offload flags and no downloads.

actions: Use disposable JARVIS_HOME, copied DB and fake dispatcher: activate empty/incompatible/complete custom model and inject stop/start failures.
oracle: Invalid model rejected before state change; every failed switch restores prior override and runtime.

actions: Load UI successfully, stop only launcher-owned backend, wait at least 6s and capture UI plus failed requests.
oracle: UI shows offline/stale within bounded time and never keeps ready from old data.

actions: Open Command Center with authenticated backend, capture WS connections/events, disconnect/reconnect and inspect event-feed state.
oracle: Transport exists or UX explicitly says disabled; terminal events are ordered/recoverable.

actions: Point UI at a local synthetic NDJSON stream that ends without terminal, then a slow stream cancelled by user/window close.
oracle: Missing terminal becomes interrupted/error; cancellation reaches backend and no late UI mutation occurs.

actions: Exercise normal/slow/disconnected/reconnected API and WebSocket flows with unique correlation IDs.
oracle: One ordered terminal state per request; no duplicates or cross-session events.

actions: With fake delayed LLM and temp append target, send two concurrent turns in one conversation; lose first HTTP response and retry same logical request.
oracle: Turns serialize and append/side effects occur exactly once using caller idempotency key.

actions: Use nested synthetic sentinel in tool environment/arguments; inject audit failure after approve/claim/finalize on copied DB, restart and query API/audit.
oracle: No sentinel persists; client/DB/audit agree; recovery cannot replay action.

actions: On temp DB make MemoryVault throw after SQL in add_memory, then perform unrelated write and reopen DB.
oracle: Failed memory is absent and later write cannot commit it.

actions: Create two benign jobs with barriers; concurrent start/update plus paused/done/already-running starts; capture rows/history/events.
oracle: No lost fields; rejected starts never publish started; every admitted run has terminal record.

actions: Use tiny temp replay ledger, evict an old committed key, change temp target and retry old transaction key.
oracle: Retry is rejected/recognized as expired and mutation is not reapplied.

actions: Use fake fetch and inject failure after digest before event/memory; also test malformed deadline and extreme cadence with unrelated due job.
oracle: Alert reconciles/retries; bad job is quarantined without blocking others.

actions: In a disposable subprocess with heartbeat, run reviewed pathological regex against small synthetic text for watch/document/archive search.
oracle: Hard budget aborts match and heartbeat/event loop remains responsive.

actions: Use mock resolver/route and local synthetic redirect graph representing public-to-private, mixed DNS and rebinding; no external targets.
oracle: Every redirect/subresource/private destination is blocked before connection.

actions: Start worker with synthetic secret-name env and sentinel runtime file; inspect effective env/mount/uid/caps/browser args; use self-signed local HTTPS and instruction sentinel.
oracle: Secrets/files are not available, sandbox is active, invalid TLS fails closed and source instructions remain data.

actions: Temp allowed root contains normal file, .env sentinel and symlink to an outside temp sentinel; call directory ingest and inspect DB/files/audit/logs.
oracle: Only intended contained files ingest; no sentinel content/path leaks.

actions: Repeat tiny ZIP/TAR/GZ/available-7z extraction with low limits into pre-snapshotted temp output.
oracle: On error output snapshot is unchanged; success publishes a complete tree atomically.

actions: Freeze clock and run repeated/concurrent same output name in temp output root.
oracle: Each success gets a unique artifact or explicit collision; no overwrite/mixed directory.

actions: Direct-run each mutating document/archive/watch tool without and with exact synthetic approval; inspect DB/files/jobs.
oracle: Policy matches documented classification; denial causes zero side effects.

actions: Copied/generated DB: corrupt locked policy JSON, simulate older schema, large histories, backup/restore, quick_check, purge and WAL checkpoint.
oracle: Policy fails closed; migrations/restore are explicit; purge semantics and bounds hold.

actions: Isolated server on alternate port with synthetic local/remote client matrix and token states; query /health only.
oracle: Unauthenticated response is minimal and never returns home/source/stored paths.

actions: Keyboard-only tabs/close/resize, focus order, reduced motion, 200-400% zoom, DPI changes, long Unicode/code/table/URL and long history.
oracle: All controls operable/labeled; no clipped critical state or focus loss.

actions: Install old then new build in test browser; test online/offline root/trace navigation and inspect CacheStorage.
oracle: New shell activates predictably; API data is never served stale from cache.

actions: Synthetic execution roots and harmless exact actions: approve/deny/cancel/retry process/file/host bridge operations; capture birth identities and cleanup.
oracle: Approval/action binding exact; denial/cancel/retry does not duplicate; no orphan resources.

actions: For all three profiles capture launcher env, model catalog, dispatcher desired/actual identity, backend status and UI label.
oracle: All five layers agree on profile/model/flags and reject unknown aliases.

actions: Repeat start/stop/restart five times on isolated project, including interrupted startup and stale state; inspect processes/containers/ports/locks.
oracle: No orphan/foreign resource, pull surprise or accumulating state; cleanup bounded.

actions: Synthetic large history/document/archive/tool output and slow disk/consumer with watchdog; record loop lag, memory, DB/WAL and render behavior.
oracle: Configured bounds/backpressure/retention hold; cancellation and health stay responsive.

label: Broad pytest excluding prohibited host-bridge modules
Frontend npm setup with unwritable default cache
Pinned frontend npm ci in disposable copy
Static contracts plus credential signature scan
